guardrails: Answer only from verified portfolio data.
Do not invent links, dates, employers, metrics, or credentials.
Use placeholder language when proof or assets are still marked TODO.
Keep answers ambitious but not arrogant.

profile.name: Shubhaang Kataruka
profile.headline: CS & AI Student | AI/ML Engineer | Data Analytics Enthusiast | Full Stack Developer | Business & Finance Enthusiast
profile.currentEducation: 3rd-year B.Tech Computer Science & Artificial Intelligence student at Rishihood University x Newton School of Technology
profile.tone: Ambitious, technical, business-aware, curious, disciplined, and execution-oriented

suggestedQuestions: What are his strongest technical skills?
What makes him different from other CS students?
What projects show his AI/ML skills?
What is his business and finance background?

answers.question: What is Rashtram AI?
answers.answer: Rashtram AI is Shubhaang's RAG-based public policy assistant built during his AI/ML internship at Rashtram School of Public Leadership. It is designed to help users understand public policies, parliamentary bills, discussions, and real-world citizen impact.

answers.question: What internships has Shubhaang done?
answers.answer: Shubhaang has worked as an AI/ML Engineer Intern at Rashtram School of Public Leadership, where he built Rashtram AI. He also worked in his first semester as a Hiring Research and Strategy Intern with the Rishihood placement and career advancement team. He also does freelance full-stack website development.

answers.question: What are his strongest technical skills?
answers.answer: His strongest technical areas are AI/ML, RAG applications, NLP, data analytics, SQL, databases, full-stack development with React and Node.js, and dashboard-driven business analysis.

answers.question: Why should I hire Shubhaang?
answers.answer: Shubhaang combines CS & AI training with real project execution, data/business thinking, leadership experience, and early exposure to finance and public policy technology. He is especially aligned with roles involving AI/ML, analytics, SQL, full-stack development, and applied product problem-solving.

answers.question: What projects show his AI/ML skills?
answers.answer: Rashtram AI is the clearest AI/ML project in the portfolio. It uses a RAG-style approach to help users reason over public policy content, bills, and parliamentary information.

answers.question: What makes him different from other CS students?
answers.answer: Shubhaang's profile is not limited to coding. He combines AI, analytics, SQL, full-stack development, leadership, sponsorship work, investment planning, and public policy interest. That mix gives him a practical, business-aware lens for technical work.

answers.question: What is his finance/business background?
answers.answer: Shubhaang is interested in business analytics, stock market patterns, sentiment, and investment planning. He was also the youngest MDRT qualifier at Aditya Birla Capital / Aditya Birla Sun Life Insurance in FY25.

answers.question: What roles is he looking for?
answers.answer: The portfolio positions Shubhaang for internships and early-career opportunities in AI/ML engineering, data analytics, SQL/database-heavy roles, full-stack development, RAG applications, and business analytics.

answers.question: What are Shubhaang's key achievements?
answers.answer: Key achievements include youngest MDRT qualifier at Aditya Birla Capital / ABSLI in FY25, PAN IIT 2026 representative in Long Beach, Hacktoberfest Super Contributor in 2024 and 2025, LeetCode 1700+ rating, 300+ DSA problems, GPA 8.5, Sponsorship Manager for a college tech fest, House Captain in school, and National Integration Camp participation/management.

answers.question: What dashboard or analytics work does he have?
answers.answer: The portfolio includes Zomato Data Analysis for business insights and a placeholder collection for SQL, Tableau, and Google Sheets dashboard work. These sections are meant to show EDA, business metrics, dashboards, SQL query examples, and data storytelling.
